---
title: "Testing, Verification, and Observability"
---

# Testing, Verification, and Observability

Range: `1140-1210`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 17    |
| stub     | 54    |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                    | Completion | Summary                                                                                                                                                       |
| ----- | --------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1140  | `STUB` [acceptance test](#rune-1140)    | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1141  | [alert](#rune-1141)                     | authored   | A threshold that wakes humans or automation when evidence crosses concern.                                                                                    |
| 1142  | `STUB` [assertion {Proof}](#rune-1142)  | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1143  | `STUB` [audit log](#rune-1143)          | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1144  | `STUB` [backtrace](#rune-1144)          | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1145  | `STUB` [baseline {Proof}](#rune-1145)   | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1146  | [benchmark](#rune-1146)                 | authored   | A performance claim forced to meet measurement. Benchmarks are where speed rhetoric either cashes out or dies.                                                |
| 1147  | `STUB` [black-box test](#rune-1147)     | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1148  | `STUB` [canary check](#rune-1148)       | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1149  | `STUB` [check](#rune-1149)              | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1150  | `STUB` [code coverage](#rune-1150)      | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1151  | `STUB` [conformance](#rune-1151)        | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1152  | [contract test](#rune-1152)             | authored   | A promise checked at the seam between systems. Use it when mocks are too flattering and end-to-end tests are too blunt.                                       |
| 1153  | `STUB` [core dump {Proof}](#rune-1153)  | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1154  | `STUB` [counter {Proof}](#rune-1154)    | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1155  | `STUB` [dashboard](#rune-1155)          | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1156  | [debugger](#rune-1156)                  | authored   | A seeing word; it arrests motion so hidden state may be inspected.                                                                                            |
| 1157  | `STUB` [diagnostic](#rune-1157)         | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1158  | `STUB` [error budget](#rune-1158)       | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1159  | `STUB` [evaluation {Proof}](#rune-1159) | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1160  | `STUB` [expectation](#rune-1160)        | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1161  | `STUB` [fixture](#rune-1161)            | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1162  | `STUB` [flame graph](#rune-1162)        | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1163  | [fuzzing](#rune-1163)                   | authored   | Throw weird inputs at the system until hidden assumptions break.                                                                                              |
| 1164  | `STUB` [gauge](#rune-1164)              | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1165  | `STUB` [golden test](#rune-1165)        | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1166  | `STUB` [harness](#rune-1166)            | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1167  | `STUB` [health probe](#rune-1167)       | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1168  | `STUB` [histogram](#rune-1168)          | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1169  | `STUB` [hypothesis](#rune-1169)         | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1170  | `STUB` [incident report](#rune-1170)    | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1171  | [integration test](#rune-1171)          | authored   | Test that multiple real parts cooperate correctly together.                                                                                                   |
| 1172  | [invariant](#rune-1172)                 | authored   | A law that must remain true while the rest of the system is allowed to move. If you cannot say the invariant, you probably do not yet understand the change.  |
| 1173  | `STUB` [load test](#rune-1173)          | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1174  | [log](#rune-1174)                       | authored   | A recorded stream of events or decisions for later reading.                                                                                                   |
| 1175  | `STUB` [logger](#rune-1175)             | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1176  | [metric](#rune-1176)                    | authored   | A numeric measure that can be tracked over time.                                                                                                              |
| 1177  | `STUB` [mock](#rune-1177)               | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1178  | `STUB` [model check](#rune-1178)        | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1179  | `STUB` [monitor](#rune-1179)            | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1180  | [observability](#rune-1180)             | authored   | Enough internal evidence exists that you can infer what the system is doing from the outside. Not logs alone: legible state under pressure.                   |
| 1181  | `STUB` [oracle](#rune-1181)             | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1182  | `STUB` [postcondition](#rune-1182)      | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1183  | `STUB` [precondition](#rune-1183)       | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1184  | [profiler](#rune-1184)                  | authored   | A measurement tool that reveals where time or allocation is being spent.                                                                                      |
| 1185  | `STUB` [proof](#rune-1185)              | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1186  | [property-based test](#rune-1186)       | authored   | Instead of checking one remembered example, ask whether a law survives many generated cases. Use it when you care more about the invariant than the anecdote. |
| 1187  | [regression test](#rune-1187)           | authored   | A test written so yesterday's bug does not return tomorrow.                                                                                                   |
| 1188  | `STUB` [replay](#rune-1188)             | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1189  | `STUB` [sampling](#rune-1189)           | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1190  | `STUB` [sanity check](#rune-1190)       | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1191  | `STUB` [simulation](#rune-1191)         | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1192  | [smoke test](#rune-1192)                | authored   | A fast confidence check that the basic path still breathes.                                                                                                   |
| 1193  | `STUB` [snapshot test](#rune-1193)      | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1194  | `STUB` [soak test](#rune-1194)          | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1195  | `STUB` [solver](#rune-1195)             | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1196  | `STUB` [span](#rune-1196)               | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1197  | `STUB` [stack trace](#rune-1197)        | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1198  | `STUB` [static checker](#rune-1198)     | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1199  | `STUB` [stress test](#rune-1199)        | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1200  | `STUB` [stub](#rune-1200)               | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1201  | `STUB` [symbolic execution](#rune-1201) | stub       | A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.                                                                                            |
| 1202  | [telemetry](#rune-1202)                 | authored   | Measurements leaving the system in a form other systems can compare, store, and alert on. Shadow: metric exhaust without explanatory power.                   |
| 1203  | `STUB` [theorem {Proof}](#rune-1203)    | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1204  | [trace {Proof}](#rune-1204)             | authored   | A preserved path through execution. Use it when the question is not only what failed, but where the causal thread first bent.                                 |
| 1205  | `STUB` [tracer](#rune-1205)             | stub       | A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.                                                                                         |
| 1206  | `STUB` [triage](#rune-1206)             | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1207  | [unit test](#rune-1207)                 | authored   | Test the smallest meaningful unit in isolation.                                                                                                               |
| 1208  | `STUB` [validation](#rune-1208)         | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |
| 1209  | `STUB` [verifier](#rune-1209)           | stub       | A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.                                                                                                     |
| 1210  | `STUB` [white-box test](#rune-1210)     | stub       | A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.                                                                          |

## Entries

### [1140] acceptance test {#rune-1140}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1141] alert {#rune-1141}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A threshold that wakes humans or automation when evidence crosses concern.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** A threshold that wakes humans or automation when evidence crosses concern.

### [1142] assertion {Proof} {#rune-1142}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1143] audit log {#rune-1143}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1144] backtrace {#rune-1144}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1145] baseline {Proof} {#rune-1145}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1146] benchmark {#rune-1146}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A performance claim forced to meet measurement. Benchmarks are where speed rhetoric either cashes out or dies.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** Force a performance claim to meet measurement.

### [1147] black-box test {#rune-1147}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1148] canary check {#rune-1148}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1149] check {#rune-1149}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1150] code coverage {#rune-1150}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1151] conformance {#rune-1151}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1152] contract test {#rune-1152}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A promise checked at the seam between systems. Use it when mocks are too flattering and end-to-end tests are too blunt.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** A promise checked at the seam between systems.

### [1153] core dump {Proof} {#rune-1153}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1154] counter {Proof} {#rune-1154}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1155] dashboard {#rune-1155}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1156] debugger {#rune-1156}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A seeing word; it arrests motion so hidden state may be inspected.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1157] diagnostic {#rune-1157}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1158] error budget {#rune-1158}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1159] evaluation {Proof} {#rune-1159}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1160] expectation {#rune-1160}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1161] fixture {#rune-1161}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1162] flame graph {#rune-1162}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1163] fuzzing {#rune-1163}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Throw weird inputs at the system until hidden assumptions break.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** Throw weird inputs at the system until hidden assumptions break.

### [1164] gauge {#rune-1164}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1165] golden test {#rune-1165}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1166] harness {#rune-1166}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1167] health probe {#rune-1167}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1168] histogram {#rune-1168}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1169] hypothesis {#rune-1169}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1170] incident report {#rune-1170}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1171] integration test {#rune-1171}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Test that multiple real parts cooperate correctly together.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** Test that multiple real parts cooperate correctly together.

### [1172] invariant {#rune-1172}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A law that must remain true while the rest of the system is allowed to move. If you cannot say the invariant, you probably do not yet understand the change.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** A law that must remain true while the rest of the system moves.

### [1173] load test {#rune-1173}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1174] log {#rune-1174}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A recorded stream of events or decisions for later reading.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** A recorded stream of events or decisions for later reading.

### [1175] logger {#rune-1175}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1176] metric {#rune-1176}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A numeric measure that can be tracked over time.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** A numeric measure that can be tracked over time.

### [1177] mock {#rune-1177}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1178] model check {#rune-1178}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1179] monitor {#rune-1179}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1180] observability {#rune-1180}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Enough internal evidence exists that you can infer what the system is doing from the outside. Not logs alone: legible state under pressure.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** Enough evidence exists to infer what the system is doing from the outside.

### [1181] oracle {#rune-1181}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1182] postcondition {#rune-1182}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1183] precondition {#rune-1183}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1184] profiler {#rune-1184}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A measurement tool that reveals where time or allocation is being spent.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** A measurement tool that reveals where time or allocation is being spent.

### [1185] proof {#rune-1185}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1186] property-based test {#rune-1186}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Instead of checking one remembered example, ask whether a law survives many generated cases. Use it when you care more about the invariant than the anecdote.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** Test the law across many generated cases instead of one remembered example.

### [1187] regression test {#rune-1187}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A test written so yesterday's bug does not return tomorrow.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** A test written so yesterday's bug does not return tomorrow.

### [1188] replay {#rune-1188}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1189] sampling {#rune-1189}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1190] sanity check {#rune-1190}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1191] simulation {#rune-1191}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1192] smoke test {#rune-1192}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A fast confidence check that the basic path still breathes.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** A fast confidence check that the basic path still breathes.

### [1193] snapshot test {#rune-1193}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1194] soak test {#rune-1194}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1195] solver {#rune-1195}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1196] span {#rune-1196}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1197] stack trace {#rune-1197}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1198] static checker {#rune-1198}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1199] stress test {#rune-1199}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1200] stub {#rune-1200}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1201] symbolic execution {#rune-1201}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A discipline word; it converts intuition into measurable evidence.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1202] telemetry {#rune-1202}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Measurements leaving the system in a form other systems can compare, store, and alert on.

**Shadow:** metric exhaust without explanatory power.

**Pocket gloss:** Measurements emitted in a form other systems can store and alert on.

### [1203] theorem {Proof} {#rune-1203}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1204] trace {Proof} {#rune-1204}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A preserved path through execution. Use it when the question is not only what failed, but where the causal thread first bent.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** A preserved path through execution.

### [1205] tracer {#rune-1205}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A revelation word; it exposes hidden state so a claim can be checked.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1206] triage {#rune-1206}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1207] unit test {#rune-1207}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Test the smallest meaningful unit in isolation.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

**Pocket gloss:** Test the smallest meaningful unit in isolation.

### [1208] validation {#rune-1208}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1209] verifier {#rune-1209}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A proving-word; it asks whether a claim is actually true.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.

### [1210] white-box test {#rune-1210}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A truth-seeking word; it narrows the gap between what is believed and what is known.

**Shadow:** the evidence surface can reward passing checks while missing the property that matters.
